title: Ipomoea laevigata
taxonomy: Ipomoea laevigata is a species of morning glory plant. It belongs to the family Convolvulaceae, which includes other types of morning glory and bindweed plants. This plant is native to Australia and is often found growing as a climber or twiner in woodland and grassland habitats. The classification of Ipomoea laevigata also includes its genus, Ipomoea, which comprises over 600 species of flowering plants. The specific epithet 'laevigata' likely refers to the plant's smooth leaves.

intro: Ipomoea laevigata, also known as the Blue Passionflower or Purple Passionflower, is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of the world.
